requested_role: Sol-style architecture review; Terra-style scenario-editor package implementation; Luna-style catalog and test wiring
summary: Added the React-free @atos/scenario-editor package, expanded registry catalog definitions, implemented immutable draft commands, validation, history, import/export, autosave, comparison, recommendation preview, and deterministic package tests.
assumptions: Issue #9 GitHub comments were not visible to the active tooling; implementation followed the issue body and accepted ADRs.
Scenario schema v1 is the active editor target.
Power recommendations from #8 are advisory unless they can be mapped to concrete scenario mutations.
known_limitations: Set-piece rotation is reported as unsupported because schema v1 has no set-piece orientation field.
Editable electrical/load metadata is not exposed because definitions do not declare editable fields.
Actual runtime model identifier was not exposed by the Codex environment.

assumptions: Browser-only file and localStorage behavior belongs in apps/web, not packages/scenario-editor.
Existing scenario-map render model should remain the shared source for SVG geometry and details.
Phone-width behavior can be validated with responsive tests plus screenshots rather than adding a separate mobile app.
known_limitations: Recommendation execution remains conservative and does not pretend unsupported #8 recommendations are scenario mutations.
The editor map is a first construction workflow, not a CAD-grade editor.

requested_role: Sol-style architecture review; Terra-style dispatch package implementation; Luna-style deterministic tests and mechanical mappings
summary: Added the @atos/dispatch package with universal dispatch chit normalization, persistent asset and worker derivation, transient super-worker formation, capability and capacity matching, deterministic guideway routing, power launch gates, resource reservations, mission plans, deficiency gates, infrastructure recommendations, seeded demand generation, and package tests.
assumptions: Issue #12 was implemented as a deterministic planning core, not movement simulation.
The extracted guideway and electrical graphs from the scenario are canonical planner inputs.
The power package is consulted only for launch allowed/delayed/blocked semantics and support asset context.
The branch is stacked on the current Issue #9 branch because packages/scenario-editor is required context and is not yet on main.
known_limitations: The exact runtime model identifier was not exposed by the Codex environment.
The pull request number was not known when this ledger entry was written.
The planner reserves discrete assets and service-zone slots but does not model time-expanded train movement, battery discharge, regenerative braking, dispatch animation, or backend persistence.

requested_role: Terra-style audit fix implementation with Sol-style architectural review boundaries
contribution_type: audit remediation, dispatch core implementation, and deterministic tests
summary: Rebased the Issue #12 branch onto main locally, added grouped dispatch manifests for compatible passenger, cargo, and mixed commuter/local cargo chits, changed reservations to use overlapping time windows with resource capacities, narrowed support-worker reservation to required zone capabilities, and expanded deterministic dispatch and web tests for the LLM audit blockers.
assumptions: Current main already contains the squashed Issue #9 editor implementation, so the Issue #12 branch can be rebased directly onto origin/main.

Compatible grouped chits must share station endpoints and overlapping ready/due windows; express passenger, long-haul cargo, hazardous cargo, perishable cargo, and bulk cargo remain separate unless grouped with the same kind.
Mixed manifests are intentionally limited to commuter passenger plus local or parcel cargo with normal stop sensitivity.
Support assets should be reserved only when their zone and capability are required by the grouped mission.
known_limitations: The exact runtime model identifier was not exposed by the Codex environment.
No browser screenshots were recaptured because the user explicitly reported Chromium does not work in this environment; no new screenshot files were added.
The planner remains deterministic and read-only; it does not simulate movement, dispatch animation, power flow over time, backend persistence, or editor behavior.
